content.full_text:
June 21, 1990

Robert E. Leidigh
Olson, Connelly, Hagel, Fong & Leidigh
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request For Confirmation of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-90-380

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

This will confirm telephone advice provided to you on May 30, 1990, regarding a candidate’s use of campaign funds to make loans to ballot measure committees under the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). Because your request does not provide specific facts with respect to a specific individual, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.

Candidates may use campaign funds to make a loan to a ballot measure committee, provided the ballot measure committee is a bona fide tax-exempt organization under Internal Revenue Code Section 501(c)(4), and the loan otherwise complies with the provisions of Section 85803. Pending adoption of a regulation by the Commission, we have conservatively advised that an organization will be considered tax-exempt when it receives written confirmation of tax-exempt status from the Internal Revenue Service.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

If you have any questions regarding the above, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Margaret W. Ellison
Counsel, Legal Division
